A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AT 1000 PM 2 MILES WNW OF LAKE BROWNWOOD STATE PARK. THIS TORNADO TRAVELED EAST...LIFTING AT 1005 PM 1 MILE WNW OF THE STATE PARK. AN NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM RATED THIS TORNADO AS AN EF-1 WITH MAXIMUM WIND SPEEDS OF 105 MPH. THE TORNADO HAD A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 175 YARDS AND WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 1.1 MILES.

*** 1 FATAL *** A MAN DROWNED WHEN HIS TRUCK WAS SWEPT OFF THE ROAD BY RUSHING WATERS APPROXIMATELY 6 MILES NORTH OF BANGS WHERE JIM NED CREEK CROSSES FM 2492 NEAR COUNTY ROAD 105. THE DRIVER WAS ABLE TO EXIT THE VEHICLE BUT WAS QUICKLY WASHED DOWNSTREAM.

*** 2 INJ *** SPOTTER CALLED WITH STRAIGHTLINE WIND DAMAGE. 1 EIGHT INCH DIAMETER TREE UPROOTED. 1 TEN INCH CEDAR TREE DOWNED. 6 VEHICLE WINDOWS BROKEN. GUTTERS RIPPED OFF STRUCTURE AND WRAPPED AROUND TREE. TWO 75 POUND POTS THROWN. LARGE SIGN THROWN INTO TRUCK...DAMAGING TRUCK. ONE WOMAN THROWN DOWN TWISTED ANGLE. ONE MAN HIT BY FLYING DEBRIS.
